name: JASMIN
oneLiner: The UK's environmental data-analysis platform, now adding high-memory and GPU-MIG capacity for machine learning.
summary: JASMIN is a specialised environmental-science data analysis facility that combines very large storage with interactive, batch, and cloud compute services. In 2026 it added high-memory nodes with 6 TB RAM and 192 CPU cores each, alongside a GPU partition that uses NVIDIA Multi-Instance GPU technology to support machine-learning workloads more efficiently. The platform is especially important for AI inference on environmental and earth-observation datasets where storage, memory, and data locality matter as much as raw accelerator count.

keyFacts.hardware: Petabyte-scale storage, new 6 TB high-memory nodes, and a GPU-MIG partition for ML workloads
keyFacts.performance: Optimised for high-memory analytics and flexible GPU slicing rather than a single headline FLOPS figure
keyFacts.energy: Shared national platform focused on efficient resource partitioning

access.route: NERC scientists access JASMIN free of charge; other users can apply on a cost-recovery basis.
